description: Letter from one of the followers of Natan b. Avraham. Dating: probably 1042 CE (GIl). The sender is seeking an appointment from the yeshiva and has met with Shelomo b. Yehuda. He complains of pressure and persecution. He is supported only by the sons of Shūʿa and two Maghribīs, Avraham b. Moshe (aka Barhūn b. Mūsā) ha-Tāhartī and Yehoshuaʿ b. Natan ha-Andalusi. Among other matters, he writes, "I had a maternal uncle (khāl) named ʿAlī ibn al-Lādhiqī. He had a feud with Solomon ben Judah [the gaon of the Yeshiva in Palestine] and could not be in the same place with him. When he died, he instructed that his body should not be taken to Jerusalem and that he should not be buried with his ancestors because of him [Solomon]. [...] They appointed him permanent ḥazzan in al-Raqqa and as muqaddam of the city. His name [i.e., patronymic] was Birabbi Joshua [=the son of R. Joshua]. He could not remain with him [with the gaon] in Jerusalem and returned to his permanent position in al-Raqqa" (Frenkel). He mentions in passing near the end the illness of the son of Yeḥezqel ha-Kohen he-Meʿulle. On verso there are astronomical calculations regarding the position of the moon. (Information in part from CUDL)
description_he: מכתב מאחד ממצדדי נתן בן אברהם, כנראה 1042. מחפש מינוי מהישיבה; נפגש עם שלמה בן יהודה. מדווח על לחץ ורדיפה. נתמך בידי בני שועא ושני מוגרבים. מספר על דודו שמת מחוץ לירושלים בשל סכסוך עם הגאון.
